page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c5.3xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 12 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 12 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8163 processor running at 2.5 GHz. The instance is configured with 24.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 2.0 GB per core ratio. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, relying on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core performance. Single-core execution is relatively weak, as shown by stress-ng and Geekbench single-core scores falling in the bottom 25%, alongside poor single-threaded gzip compression. However, multi-core performance is consistently average, placing in the middle 50% for stress-ng multi-core, Passmark CPU, and Geekbench multi-core tests. Memory bandwidth is also average, reaching up to 493.85 GB/sec for cached reads and around 58.83 GB/sec for uncached large blocks.

The primary tradeoff of the ecs.c5.3xlarge is its low memory-to-vCPU ratio and weak single-threaded throughput, which makes it less suitable for memory-intensive databases or single-threaded legacy applications. Qualitatively, it offers a resource-dense compute profile for multi-threaded workloads that do not require local NVMe storage. It is well-suited for parallelizable tasks such as software compilation, multi-threaded decompression, static web serving, and lightweight model inference, where its 12 dedicated physical cores can be fully utilized.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c5.3xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 12 dedicated vCPUs on an Intel Xeon 8163 processor running at 2.5 GHz. Equipped with 24.0 GB of RAM, it provides a 1:2 vCPU-to-memory ratio, with no local storage or GPU resources. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, the instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps. Performance benchmarks show weak single-core capabilities but average multi-core and memory bandwidth results, making it a balanced choice for parallel processing. The primary tradeoff is its low memory allocation per core and weak single-threaded performance. It is best suited for multi-threaded workloads such as software compilation, static web serving, and moderate multi-threaded decompression tasks.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c5.3xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 12 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 24 GB RAM, and 4 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering average multi-core performance for parallel workloads.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c5.3xlarge is a KVM-based compute-optimized instance featuring 12 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 24 GB RAM.
tagline: A dedicated twelve-core compute instance with a one-to-two processor-to-memory ratio, optimized for parallelized, multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 12 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs with 24.0 GB of system memory
Average multi-core performance across CPU and memory benchmarks
Baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps with zero local storage
Weak single-core and single-threaded execution speeds
Best suited for software compilation, static web serving, and multi-threaded tasks
